news.title: Indore tops in generating revenue from property registration
news.para: Madhya Pradesh's stamp duty and property registration earnings surged by 12.82% in the first two months of the fiscal year, reaching Rs 1,843.54 crore. This growth, driven by higher guideline values and property prices, outpaced a modest 4% increase in transaction volumes. Indore led revenue collections with Rs 328.45 crore.

news.title: IT Park in Mangaluru tender draws no bids, deadline extended
news.para: Mangaluru's Rs 135 crore IT Park project faces another delay as no bidders have emerged, forcing an extension of the tender deadline. Poor connectivity due to a narrow approach road and restrictive tender conditions, including high deposit requirements and consortium limitations, are cited as major deterrents by potential investors.

news.title: Lucknow: Fire department issues notices to 20 hotels for fire safety violations
news.para: Lucknow's fire department has issued notices to twenty hotels and restaurants following a citywide inspection. Twelve establishments have been given forty-five days to fix structural deficiencies. The inspections revealed critical gaps in emergency exits, staircases, and firefighting systems. Officials are emphasizing public safety and will take strict action against persistent violations.

news.title: Two builders held for building collapse in Delhi that killed six; probe to examine design, illegal extra floors
news.para: Delhi Police have apprehended a builder and contractor following a building collapse that killed six and injured eight. The arrested individuals, Avinash Gupta and Manish Khatri, are being questioned about the unauthorized construction of two extra floors on a structure originally designed for four.

news.title: Nagpur identifies 278 dilapidated buildings, demolishes only 67 before monsoon
news.para: Monsoon is approaching Nagpur, but many unsafe buildings remain standing. The Nagpur Municipal Corporation identified 278 dilapidated structures for 2025-26, yet only 67 have been demolished. Hundreds of families are at risk from potential collapses. Gandhibagh zone has the highest number of unsafe buildings. Action is delayed by legal and ownership issues.

news.title: Gurugram: Buyers of affordable project stalled since 2016 see red in email claim
news.para: Homebuyers of the stalled Expressway Towers project in Gurugram have accused officials of misusing government email channels. They claim emails from a small group opposing a project takeover were forwarded through official channels. This influenced decisions regarding the delayed project. Buyers are facing financial strain from rent and EMIs. They urge authorities to expedite a decision for project completion.

news.title: Hyderabad: HMDA plan to auction land worth ₹1,000 crore in Banjara Hills hit by row over ownership
news.para: A high-stakes land auction by HMDA in Banjara Hills faces controversy as two claimants allege overlap with their holdings. Yamuna Nagar Cooperative House Building Society and Theme Ambience Construction Pvt Ltd are seeking a demarcation of the 8.24-acre government parcel, valued at Rs 1,000 crore, before the June 19 auction.

news.title: Flat buyers entitled to interest under RERA for each month’s delay: Bombay HC
news.para: Bombay High Court ruled that homebuyers have an unqualified right to receive monthly interest for delayed possession under Rera. Justice Madhav Jamdar emphasized the law's intent to protect buyers from developer delays, stating they are unconditionally entitled to interest until possession is granted. The court cited buyers' vulnerable position as financiers of projects.

news.title: ITAT deletes ₹14 lakh ‘on-money’ addition against Thane flat buyer
news.para: A Thane resident's Rs 14.25 lakh tax addition for alleged cash payment on a flat purchase was deleted by the ITAT. The tribunal ruled the Income Tax department failed to provide evidence linking the buyer to 'on-money' transactions, emphasizing that statements alone aren't sufficient proof without cross-examination.

news.title: ED attaches ₹100 crore worth properties in Jaypee, claims Gaur laundered them
news.para: In a significant crackdown on financial misconduct, the Enforcement Directorate has seized assets worth Rs 100 crore from two real estate firms, including Jaypee Infratech Ltd (JIL). Authorities accuse JIL and its leadership of misappropriating homebuyers' investments. Investigations have unveiled a staggering Rs 33,000 crore was diverted from the Jaypee Wish Town projects for purposes unrelated to construction.

news.title: No demolition of existing colonies in O-Zone area: Delhi CM
news.para: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta has assured residents that existing homes in O-Zone areas will not face demolition. The High Court order specifically targets new or ongoing construction. Public representatives confirmed this, stating existing structures are safe. The administration will revise signage to prevent panic. A ban on new construction in the O-Zone is acceptable.

news.title: Wadhwa Group leases 33,000 sq ft office space in Mumbai for ₹44 crore rental in nine-year period
news.para: Wadhwa Group has leased nearly 33,000 sq ft of office space to Morningstar in its Vishwaroop IT Park, Vashi, Mumbai. This nine-year lease agreement is valued at Rs 44 crore, increasing Morningstar's total footprint in the park to 4.45 lakh sq ft. The commercial asset is currently fully leased, generating an annual rental income of around Rs 75 crore.

news.title: Tamil Nadu to assign guideline values to all land survey numbers by June-end
news.para: Tamil Nadu's Commercial Taxes Minister T. Logesh Tamilselvan has mandated that all land survey numbers in the state receive a market guideline value by the end of June. He also urged officials to prioritize faster, citizen-friendly registration services, ensuring courteous treatment, prompt document returns, and efficient resolution of pending files to minimize public complaints.

news.title: UK homebuilder Bellway warns of slowing demand, rising energy costs
news.para: British homebuilder Bellway reported a cooling in customer demand and rising building costs due to higher energy prices, despite maintaining annual profit and volume targets. Soaring energy prices have impacted input materials, affecting UK builders and government housing targets. The company's private reservation rate fell, though incentives helped mitigate the impact.

news.title: Odisha cabined approves reduction in stamp duty on houses for EWS
news.para: Odisha's state cabinet has significantly reduced stamp duty and registration fees for Economically Weaker Section beneficiaries of PMAY-U 2.0 and state housing schemes. Charges will now range from 0.6% to 0.7%, a substantial drop from the current 6-7%, aiming to boost housing uptake and streamline the Centre's urban housing program.

news.title: NGT panel flags 20 bungalows in Pune’s Moi Phata inside Indrayani flood line; demolition likely
news.para: Twenty homes in Moi Phata are under scrutiny for flood-line violations. These structures are located along the Ram Zara nullah, a tributary of the Indrayani river. The nullah itself falls within flood lines, raising concerns about water flow obstruction. The National Green Tribunal will hear the matter soon. This situation could lead to demolitions, similar to past actions in Chikhali.

news.title: ED conducts fresh raids in Punjab, Uttar Pradesh in Sanjeev Arora case
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has conducted fresh searches in a money laundering probe related to an alleged Rs 100 crore GST fraud. The investigation involves Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora and others, with searches spanning multiple locations in Punjab and Uttar Pradesh. Arora was arrested last month and is currently in judicial custody.

news.title: Construction material prices surge, Chennai developers warn of costlier homes
news.para: Construction material costs have surged over 23%, potentially increasing house prices by 10% due to the West Asian conflict and a supply crunch from quarry closures. Industry stakeholders warn this could be passed on to homebuyers, impacting sales and jobs. Developers are urging the government to ensure uninterrupted supply of legally sourced materials and regulate prices.

news.title: Ten years on, Telangana’s Lifts Act still stuck in limbo
news.para: Telangana is yet to enforce its Lifts Act, despite a decade-old proposal and recent fatal accidents. The High Court has questioned the delay, while experts highlight the need for uniform safety standards to prevent tragedies, especially for vulnerable groups. The proposed act aims to regulate installations, maintenance, and inspections, mirroring regulations in ten other Indian states.

news.title: After 32 years, VDA to launch three mega townships in Varanasi
news.para: Varanasi Development Authority is launching three integrated townships, including a Sports City, marking its first major initiative since 1994. Backed by Rs 400 crore seed capital, these projects aim to boost planned urban development. The land-pooling model is being adopted, transforming farmers into development partners.
